title: Fluffy Citrus Spanish Cauliflower Paella
description: A keto-friendly, citrusy paella where cauliflower takes the place of rice, fluffy thanks to a creamy mayo blend and a touch of saffron, delivering indulgent flavor with a Spanish twist.
instructions: Soak the saffron threads in 1 tbsp hot water for 5 minutes to release flavor.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet or paella pan over medium-high heat. Add cauliflower florets and sauté ~4 minutes until lightly browned. Transfer to a plate.
In the same pan, add diced onion and cook 2 minutes until translucent. Add minced garlic and cook 30 seconds.
Stir in ground paprika, cooking just until fragrant (~20 seconds).
Add the saffron infusion and stir to combine.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring to a gentle simmer. Let it reduce slightly for about 5 minutes.
Return the cauliflower to the pan, stirring to coat with the broth and spices.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes until cauliflower is tender and the liquid has mostly absorbed.
While the cauliflower cooks, whisk mayonnaise with lemon juice, lemon zest, salt, and pepper until smooth.
When the cauliflower is cooked, fluff it gently with a fork, then stir in the mayo mixture until the dish becomes creamy and fluffy.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot.
